bibliographic_data.title: Phase transitions in the $q$-coloring of random hypergraphs
bibliographic_data.abstract: We study in this paper the structure of solutions in the random hypergraph coloring problem and the phase transitions they undergo when the density of constraints is varied. Hypergraph coloring is a constraint satisfaction problem where each constraint includes K variables that must be assigned one out of q colors in such a way that there are no monochromatic constraints, i.e., there are at least two distinct colors in the set of variables belonging to every constraint. This problem generalizes naturally coloring of random graphs (K = 2) and bicoloring of random hypergraphs (q = 2), both of which were extensively studied in past works.

core_content.novelty_claim: The study provides explicit values and predictions for phase transitions that were discovered in other constraint satisfaction problems but never evaluated before in hypergraph coloring, including revisiting the bicoloring problem (q = 2) with K = 3 and K = 4.
core_content.key_methodology: Statistical mechanics methods are applied to random hypergraph coloring for the first time, considering arbitrary values of K and q.
outcomes: Explicit values and predictions for phase transitions in hypergraph coloring
Revisited bicoloring problem (q = 2) with K = 3 and K = 4, finding that the colorability threshold is not given by one-step-replica-symmetry-breaking analysis
Unveiled coexistence of two different 1RSB solutions in the case of q = 2, K ≥ 4
core_content.full_text_markdown: In this paper, we investigate phase transitions in the random hypergraph coloring problem. Hypergraph coloring is a constraint satisfaction problem where each constraint includes K variables that must be assigned one out of q colors such that no monochromatic constraints exist. This generalizes graph coloring (K = 2) and bicoloring of random hypergraphs (q = 2). The study provides explicit values and predictions for phase transitions, including revisiting the bicoloring problem with K = 3 and K = 4, where the colorability threshold is not given by one-step-replica-symmetry-breaking analysis. Additionally, we uncover coexistence of two different 1RSB solutions in the case of q = 2, K ≥ 4.
